steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a transitions question. The passage opens by saying activists statewide sold tea for suffrage, then gives one example (San Francisco). The blank introduces a second parallel example (Los Angeles). The transition must signal similarity between two independent, coordinated examples.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "For example,": Signals that the second sentence exemplifies the first. But both sentences are distinct examples of the same general claim — one does not illustrate the other. ✗
  • "To conclude,": Signals a closing summary. The passage is not wrapping up; it is adding a parallel case. ✗
  • "Similarly,": Signals that two things are alike. Helen Pierce Davis distributed tea in Los Angeles just as the Woman’s Suffrage Party sold tea in San Francisco — a parallel action in a parallel city. ✓
  • "In other words,": Signals restatement or paraphrase. The Los Angeles example is a new, distinct event, not a restatement of the San Francisco one. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
